items.title: Spark AI
items.content:
I joined Spark AI as a Senior Frontend Developer in July 2023, became Team Lead in August 2024, and transitioned to Platform Architect in November 2025.

I lead a cross-functional team of eight, including developers, designers, and QA engineers. My role focuses on high-level architecture, strategic planning, and design validation.

I engineered core authentication workflows and a custom Prompt Studio tool for Prompt Privacy, and refactored the Ethera codebase.

By enforcing strict clean code standards and optimization patterns, I reduced project bundle sizes by 20% and maintained a 100% on-time sprint delivery rate.

items.title: Globewyze
items.content:
From December 2022 to June 2023, I worked as a Frontend Developer optimizing the SwipeFox platform.

I introduced consistent coding guidelines and modular structural patterns, improving codebase maintainability by 15%.

Using Redux and Ant Design, I resolved complex state synchronization and interaction issues, driving a 50% increase in system functionality.

I also built a custom video player matching pixel-perfect Figma specifications for user video introductions. I managed sprints and dependencies within Jira to ensure predictable, structured delivery.

items.title: Million News Media
items.content:
As a React.js Developer from April to November 2022, I built the frontend architecture for StocksTelegraph using Next.js, Tailwind CSS, and React Query.

I developed an efficient, reusable component library that accelerated quote page creation by 50%.

To optimize performance and visibility, I architected a server-side rendering (SSR) strategy for high-traffic data pages and implemented shallow routing.

This technical execution resulted in a 55% boost in SEO performance, search rankings, and user engagement metrics.

items.title: Alpha Squad
items.content:
In March 2022, I started my professional career at Alpha Squad as an Associate Frontend Developer.

I built UI components for the Moda Admin template using HTML, CSS, and vanilla JavaScript.

Simultaneously, I used rapid self-directed learning to master React.js, Next.js, and Tailwind CSS.

By introducing utility-first styling patterns and streamlining the design-to-code asset pipeline, I increased internal development workflow efficiency by 30% within my first month.

items.title: Frontend
items.content:
My core technical stack centers on JavaScript, TypeScript, React.js, and Next.js.

TypeScript is a non-negotiable part of my workflow for catching compilation errors and ensuring type safety.

I specialize in component-driven architecture within React and leverage Next.js to implement optimized rendering strategies like SSR and SSG.

I prioritize structural clean code and clean architecture over simply using trendy tools, ensuring applications scale predictably.

items.title: UI Libraries
items.content:
For interface design, I primarily use Tailwind CSS, including v4, paired with shadcn/ui for accessible, unstyled component foundations.

I am also experienced with Bootstrap and Material UI (MUI).

My data layer architecture relies on React Query for declarative server-state caching and synchronization, Redux Toolkit for centralized client state management, and Axios for HTTP communications.

I evaluate every external dependency strictly as a long-term codebase commitment.

items.title: Dev Tools
items.content:
I manage my local engineering environment on Apple silicon hardware, currently using a MacBook Pro M1.

For version control, I maintain a strict Git workflow with structured branching and semantic commit history.

I use ESLint to automate code quality and styling guidelines across teams.

For project management and tracking cross-functional deliverables, I adapt to either Jira or Trello, optimizing workflows to minimize administrative overhead.

items.title: Overview
items.content:
I am Mohtashim Ali, a Frontend Developer and Platform Architect based in Pakistan with over four years of professional experience.

I build fast, optimized, and architecturally sound web applications.

What drives me is engineering clean, maintainable systems that solve complex performance challenges.

Currently, I am expanding my backend and data knowledge by building a personal finance app using Next.js, and SQLite to automate transaction tracking.

items.title: Life
items.content:
Outside of software architecture, I focus on performance-driven hardware and technology optimization.

I play deep high-graphics PC and mobile titles like *Wuthering Waves*, *Punishing: Gray Raven*, and *Arknights: Endfield*.

I benchmarked and assembled a custom AMD Ryzen rig optimized for a 1440p high-refresh-rate monitor.

I also engineered and optimized a 4kW home solar inverter setup with modern lithium battery storage.

Currently, I am researching EV commuter options from Yadea and Metro. I value family and enjoy planning travel and activities with my wife.

items.title: High School
items.content:
From 2016 to 2018, I completed my FSc Pre-Engineering at the Pak Kashmir Institute of Computer Sciences in Jhelum, Punjab.

My coursework focused heavily on physics and advanced mathematics.

This academic track built the foundational logical reasoning, core analytical frameworks, and structured problem-solving skills that made my later transition into software engineering natural and intuitive.

items.title: University
items.content:
I graduated with a B.Sc. in Software Engineering from the University of Engineering & Technology (UET) in 2022.

The program provided a rigid grounding in data structures, algorithms, and system design patterns.

Parallel to my studies, I built and shipped production projects, allowing me to instantly validate academic engineering theory against real-world codebases.

items.title: Overview
items.content:
My engineering philosophy extends into my personal life.

I use my free time to recharge my mental focus by analyzing and optimizing complex real-world hardware systems.

Stepping away from the screen allows me to return to application architecture with fresh analytical perspectives.

items.title: Gaming
items.content:
I play mechanically deep, high-fidelity titles including *Wuthering Waves*, *Punishing: Gray Raven*, and *Arknights: Endfield*.

I enjoy analyzing combat mechanics, structural optimization, and tweaking engine settings to maximize stable frame rates on my hardware.

items.title: Hardware and Solar
items.content:
I build and optimize physical infrastructure.

This includes an automated 4kW home solar power installation paired with lithium battery management, and a custom AMD desktop build matched to a QHD 1440p monitor setup.

items.title: Research Rabbit Holes
items.content:
I take a data-driven approach to consumer technology.

I spend weeks analyzing benchmarks, specification sheets, and regional pricing before making any asset decisions.

My current focus is evaluating local EV bikes, comparing peak motor power and battery degradation cycles.
